_comment: Capitalized game-term glossary, EN -> pt-BR. Add every capitalized rules term encountered while translating compendium content. Keep capitalization in translated text matching the English source (proper-noun style). Terms marked [lang file] are pulled directly from lang/pt-BR.json for consistency with the UI; terms marked [compendium-only] don't appear in the UI and were coined here for prose consistency.
Undeads: Mortos-Vivos [lang file BeingTypes.Undead=Morto-Vivo, pluralized] (bestiary folder name)
Outers: Exteriores [lang file BeingTypes.Outers, exact match] (bestiary folder name)
Cryptids: Criptídeos [lang file BeingTypes.Cryptid=Criptídeo, pluralized] (bestiary folder name)
Primordials: Primordiais [lang file BeingTypes.Primordials, exact match] (bestiary folder name)
Artificials: Artificiais [lang file BeingTypes.Artificials, exact match] (bestiary folder name)
Fae: Feéricos [lang file BeingTypes.Fae=Feérico, pluralized] (bestiary folder name)
Beasts: Bestas [lang file BeingTypes.Beasts, exact match — NOT 'Feras', that's the singular prose tag 'Beast' used inside monster descriptions, different context] (bestiary folder name)
Specials: Especiais (bestiary folder name, not an official beingType — coined, no lang-file precedent)
Wyrm: Wyrm (kept as fantasy loanword, no PT-BR precedent, compendium-only creature-subtype prose tag e.g. "(Cryptid, Wyrm)")
Sight: Visão (compendium-only, NPC action delivery type, coined — no lang-file DeliveryTypes.Sight precedent, distinct from 'Sunlight')
Sunlight: Luz do Sol (compendium-only, bestiary recurring hazard for Undead/Vampire-type creatures)
All-Sight: Visão Total [compendium-only] (system.senses value — source inconsistently hyphenates "All-Sight"/"Allsight", both mean the same, translate identically)
Far (adjective, in shape+range combos): Distante — used ONLY when Far modifies a shape noun directly ("Far Cone"->"Cone Distante", "Aura Far"->"Aura Distante"); standalone Far stays "Longe" per existing dictionary entry. Mirrors Near's existing Próximo/Próxima adjective flex.
Nightwalker: Andarilho Noturno (compendium-only, recurring Undead ability — reuse this exact string every time, description also fixed: "Não pode se Mover ou ter Alvos sobre linhas de sal ou em áreas iluminadas pela Luz do Sol. Fica Incapacitado e Queimando (d8) enquanto estiver sob a Luz do Sol.")
Zombie (ability name): Zumbi — recurring Undead resilience trait ("can't be reduced below 1 HP unless..."), same word as the creature type, reuse fixed description: "Não pode ser reduzido abaixo de 1 PV a menos que sofra dano de um Crítico, dano ao qual é Fraco, ou esteja sob a Luz do Sol."
Fear Aura: Aura de Medo (compendium-only, recurring Undead ability, fixed description: "[Aura Próxima | Vontade]: Amedrontado até passar neste Teste em um Turno subsequente, ou Cd6 depois.")
Magic Ward <N>: Resistência Mágica <N> (compendium-only ability, e.g. Magic Ward II->Resistência Mágica II — deliberately NOT reusing the Ward spell's "Proteção" translation, different concept: magic resistance vs. the Ward spell)
Terror <N>: Terror <N> (kept, Roman numeral tier suffix, e.g. Terror II/III — cognate)
Church Grim: Church Grim (kept as loanword — obscure British folklore term, no PT-BR precedent, monster proper name)
Lich: Lich (kept as loanword, no clean PT-BR equivalent, monster proper name)
Wight: Wight (kept as loanword, no clean PT-BR equivalent, monster proper name — do not confuse with Warg)
Mummy: Múmia (monster proper name); Mummy Lord -> Múmia Suprema
Banshee: Banshee (kept as loanword, already an English/Irish loanword in PT fantasy media too)
Bolt: DEPRECATED — was inconsistent flavor term for the Remote delivery type; all NPC action notes normalized to "Remote"/"Remoto" 2026-07-18. Don't reintroduce.
Chain: Cota de Malha (armor material flavor term, compendium-only, NPC armorDescription)
Leather: Couro (armor material flavor term, compendium-only, NPC armorDescription)
Plate: Placas (armor material flavor term, compendium-only, NPC armorDescription)
Unarmored: Sem Armadura (compendium-only, NPC armorDescription — distinct from armor.json's Light/Medium/Heavy category names)
Targeted: Alvo (adjective, e.g. "Targeted Objects" -> "Objetos Alvo", "Targeted Beings" -> "Criaturas Alvo")
Item: Item [lang file: kept as English loanword]
Save/Saves: Salvaguarda/Salvaguardas [lang file, singular corrected 2026-07-18 from 'Resistência' -- 'Resistência' is reserved for the Endure save's own name (see Endure entry) and elemental-resistance uses; the generic word for 'a Save' is 'Salvaguarda', matching lang/pt-BR.json's own SaveRoll: 'Salvaguarda']
Endure: Resistir [save type, user-corrected 2026-07-18 from 'Resistência' -- clashed with generic 'Save' (see Save/Saves entry). Two-step fix: (1) 'Resistir' names the Endure save type specifically, (2) generic 'Save' noun corrected from 'Resistência' to 'Salvaguarda' so it no longer collides with anything. 'Resistência' is now free for elemental-resistance/Magic Ward uses only, never a save term. Propagated across all packs: bare tag form (bestiary.json/humanlike.json 'note' fields, e.g. '| Resistir') stays untouched by the Salvaguarda change -- compact stat-block tags use the bare save-type name, not 'Salvaguarda de X'. Also fixed a stray mistranslation found along the way: perks.json Patience perk had 'Resistência de Vigor' for source 'Endure Save' -- 'Vigor' was wrong, corrected to 'Salvaguarda de Resistir'.]
Hulking (Orc trait): Corpulento [compendium-only, already coined via effects entry]
Naturally Attuned (Elf trait): Naturalmente Sintonizado [compendium-only, already coined via effects entry]
Breath Attack (Draken trait): Ataque de Sopro [already coined in vagabond.weapons.json]
Scale (Draken trait): Escama [compendium-only, already coined via effects entry]
Relic power categories (compendium-only,: Movement->Movimento, Utility->Utilidade, Senses->Sentidos, Bonus->Bônus, Cursed->Amaldiçoado, Fabled->Lendário, Resistance->Resistência, Protection->Proteção, Bane->Flagelo, Ace->Ás, Strike->Golpe
Imperial volume units in gear descriptio: pint->pinta, gallon->galão, ounce->onça (kept as translated words, not converted to metric, consistent with feet/lb staying imperial elsewhere)
Detect (skill): Detectar [lang file], Detect Check -> Teste de Detecção [compendium-only]
Breather: Recuperar (o) Fôlego [lang file: VAGABOND.Downtime.Breather.Title, verb usage 'takes a Breather'->'Recupera o Fôlego']
Shift (time-block unit, NOT combat Turn): Turno [lang file: VAGABOND.Downtime.Cost.OneShift — CAUTION: official pt-BR reuses 'Turno' for both combat Turn and this longer downtime Shift block, a pre-existing ambiguity in the shipped localization, not introduced by this translation pass]
Rank (potion/countdown-die power tier): Grau [compendium-only — deliberately distinct from Level->Nível to avoid clashing with character Level]
Craft (verb, 'to Craft an item'): Fabricar [compendium-only, distinct from the Craft skill noun->Ofício]
Blink (spell + relic power, short-range: Lampejo [compendium-only, user-corrected 2026-07-18 from 'Piscar' -- Piscar means eyelid-blink, wrong sense for a teleport effect. Propagated to spells.json Blink entry, relics.json 'Blink Dagger'/'Movement - Blinking']
Bind/Bond (verb, relic attunement): Vincular [lang file, confirmed via grep], Unbind->Desvincular, Bound (adj/past)->Vinculado, the Bond/Vinculação (requiresBound label 'Requer Vinculação')
X Save (single named save, e.g. 'Endure: Reflex Save->Salvaguarda de Reflexo, Will Save->Salvaguarda de Vontade, Endure Save->Salvaguarda de Resistir [head noun corrected 2026-07-18 from 'Resistência' to 'Salvaguarda', see Save/Saves entry]. Combined save phrasing ('X or Y Save' prose, e.g. Draken breath / weapons.json Breath Attack): 'Salvaguarda de Resistir ou Vontade'. Compact NPC stat-block tags (bestiary.json/humanlike.json 'note' fields, after the '|') stay bare, no 'Salvaguarda de' prefix -- e.g. 'Resistir ou Reflexo', 'Reflexo ou Resistir', matching the existing tag-style precedent.
All-Sight/Allsight (relic sense Power): Visão Total [compendium-only, coined to match 'Visão no Escuro'/'Visão Cega' SensesPlaceholder pattern]
Tarot Major Arcana card names (Deck of M: Standard Brazilian Portuguese Rider-Waite names used: Justice->Justiça, The World->O Mundo, The Empress->A Imperatriz, The Lovers->Os Enamorados, The Devil->O Diabo, The Fool->O Louco, Wheel of Fortune->A Roda da Fortuna, Temperance->Temperança, The Hierophant->O Hierofante, The High Priestess->A Sacerdotisa, Strength->A Força, The Magician->O Mago, The Moon->A Lua, The Hanged Man->O Enforcado, The Tower->A Torre, Death->A Morte, The Star->A Estrela, The Sun->O Sol, Judgement->O Julgamento, The Emperor->O Imperador, The Hermit->O Eremita, The Chariot->O Carro
Lore (system.lore field label, relic-onl: Info [compendium-only, user-corrected 2026-07-18 from 'Lenda' -- Lenda means literal legend/myth, wrong sense: field actually holds a short item-type tag ('Relic Boots') or one-line rule blurb ('Enhances a Weapon Property.'), not narrative history, despite the field's doc-comment/placeholder implying flavor text. lang/pt-BR.json FIELDS.lore.label + RelicLorePlaceholder both updated.
Fabled (item rarity tier, distinct from: Lendário/Lendária [compendium-only, agrees with noun gender -- Lendário for masc nouns (Elmo, Revólver, Escudo, Item, Martelo, Tridente, Baralho), Lendária for fem (Espada, Adaga)]
Studied die/dice (Study downtime mechani: Dado de Estudo/Dados de Estudo [lang file: VAGABOND.Hud.StudiedTooltip full phrasing; UI abbreviation is 'D. Estudo']
Breather (noun, 'during a Breather'): Recuperação de Fôlego [compendium-only nominalization; the system's own action label is the verb phrase 'Recuperar Fôlego' (VAGABOND.Downtime.Breather.Title), which doesn't work as a bare noun in prose]
Countdown die/dice: Dado de Contagem [lang file: VAGABOND.CountdownDice.Create/Delete use 'Dado de Contagem']
Quest: Missão [compendium-only, coined, no lang-file precedent found]
Off-Turn (prose only, no system field): fora do [seu] Turno [compendium-only, rendered as a phrase not a fixed noun]
Erguer/Guiar/Polimorfizar/Congelar/Levit: Raise->Reanimar, Guide->Guiar, Polymorph->Polimorfia, Freeze->Congelar, Levitate->Levitar, Sprout->Brotar, Ward->Proteção, Life->Vida, Apoplex->Apoplexia [pulled from translationWork/vagabond.spells.json, not re-coined]
Boomer (bestiary monster type, p.181): Boomer [kept untranslated -- Bestiary pack not yet translated as of this perks pass, no official term exists yet; revisit once Bestiary/Humanlike packs are done]
Beyonder (creature subtype flavor tag, e: Alienígena [compendium-only, coined — cosmic-horror/Lovecraftian creature subtype tag shown in system.description, distinct from the beingType Outers/Exteriores]
Regenerate <N> (recurring NPC ability): Regenerar <N> [compendium-only, e.g. Regenerate II->Regenerar II, coined this pass — first appeared in Outers]
Kinesis (Ogler eyebeam suboption, teleki: Telecinesia [compendium-only, expanded to full form for clarity]
Instant Transmission (Byakhee ability, t: Teletransporte Instantâneo [compendium-only, matches the common PT-BR fan term for this trope]
Sizes (system.size / prose size referenc: small->Pequeno, medium->Médio, large->Grande, huge->Enorme, giant->Gigante, colossal->Colossal [lang file: VAGABOND.Sizes.*]
Outers session-2 monster proper-name tra: Deep One->Profundo, Demon Ray->Raia Demoníaca, Dimension Ripper->Rasgador Dimensional, False Hydra->Hidra Falsa, Floating Eye->Olho Flutuante, Gibbering Mouther->Boca Tagarela, Hellhound->Cão Infernal, Imp->Diabrete, Nightmare->Pesadelo, Ogler->Observador [coined, root 'olhar'=to stare/ogle], Pit Fiend->Demônio do Abismo, Viper Tree->Árvore Víbora, Wolf in Sheep's Clothing->Lobo em Pele de Cordeiro [existing PT-BR idiom, exact match], Phoenix Down->Pena de Fênix [matches common PT-BR localization of this trope]. Kept as loanwords/proper nouns (obscure folklore or invented D&D-style coinages, same precedent as Lich/Wight/Banshee): Agnar, Byakhee, Chort, Cloaker, Couatl, Dethbat, Oni, Otyugh, Stolas (Demônio Stolas), Thoom, Thulhan, Vigzud, Viskyd, Zotz (Demônio Zotz). Not user-confirmed yet, same as prior packs' name calls.
Magic Resistance <N> (Gremlin ability, d: Resiliência Mágica <N> [compendium-only, coined 2026-07-18 -- deliberately DISTINCT from 'Magic Ward <N>'->'Resistência Mágica <N>', a different mechanic (extra Mana cost vs. Sight roll-to-ignore); reusing the same PT phrase for both would conflate them]
Hide (armor material, Carbuncle only): Pele [compendium-only, coined 2026-07-18 -- distinct material from Leather->Couro]
Tendril (Roper limb, distinct English wo: Tentáculo [compendium-only, reuses the same translation as Tentacle -- both are grappling-limb attacks (Kraken/Froghemoth), no distinct PT-BR term needed]
Cutlass (weapon, Wererat): Alfanje [compendium-only, standard PT word for this curved blade]
Cursed (status, turn-to-stone effects): Amaldiçoado [compendium-only, coined 2026-07-18 -- no lang-file StatusConditions.Cursed entry found, used for Basilisk/Cockatrice/Medusa petrification]
Cryptids session-3 monster proper-name t: Amphiptere->Anfíptero, Basilisk->Basilisco, Carbuncle->Carbúnculo, Carcass Crawler->Devorador de Carcaças, Cave Fisher->Pescador de Caverna, Choker->Estrangulador, Cockatrice->Cocatriz, Darkmantle->Manto Sombrio, Dragon Turtle->Tartaruga Dragão, Dragon tiers: Ancient->Ancião, Elder->Idoso, Hatchling->Filhote, Young->Jovem (colors Black/Blue/Green/Red/White->Preto/Azul/Verde/Vermelho/Branco), Flail Snail->Caracol Mangual, Griffon->Grifo, Harpy->Harpia, Hippocampus->Hipocampo, Hippogriff->Hipogrifo, Hydra->Hidra, Manticore->Mantícora, Medusa->Medusa (unchanged), Mimic->Mímico, Minotaur->Minotauro, Ogre->Ogro, Owl Bear->Urso-Coruja, Peryton->Periton, Piercer->Perfurador, Pseudodragon->Pseudodragão, Purple Worm->Verme Púrpura, Questing Beast->Besta Fera [Arthurian legend's established PT-BR term], Roc->Roca, Rot Grub->Larva Podre, Rust Monster->Monstro da Ferrugem, Sea Lion->Leão do Mar, Sea Serpent->Serpente Marinha, Snakeman->Homem-Serpente, Stirge->Estirge, Tarrasque->Tarrasca, Troglodyte->Troglodita, Warp Beast->Fera Distorcedora, Wererat->Homem-Rato, Werewolf->Lobisomem (Dire->Terrível), Chimera->Quimera. Kept as loanwords (obscure cryptid folklore/invented D&D terms, same precedent as Lich/Wight/Banshee): Achaierai, Agropelter, Almiraj, Amikuk, Ankheg, Aurumvorax, Bugbear, Bulette, Catoblepas, Dobar-Chú, Drake, Ettercap, Froghemoth, Gremlin, Hobgoblin, Hodag, Jarjacha, Kobold, Kraken, Lindworm, Nymit, Orc, Rhagodessa, Roper, Rumptifusel, Skeljaskrimsli, Snallygaster, Wyvern, Yeti, Goblin, Giant. Not user-confirmed yet, same as prior packs' name calls.
Immutable (recurring construct/undead ab: Imutável (compendium-only, "It can't be transformed."->"Não pode ser transformado/a.")
Acid Bather / Fire-Absorber / Shock-Abso: Banhista de Ácido / Absorve-Fogo / Absorve-Choque [coined, compendium-only]
Material-comma naming convention for Gol: Kept source comma structure, translated material word only: Golem materials Bone->Osso, Clay->Argila, Flesh->Carne, Iron->Ferro, Stone->Pedra. Living Statue materials Crystal->Cristal, Iron->Ferro, Rock->Rocha. Same convention as Elemental, X pattern from session 4.
Artificials session-5 monster proper-nam: Animated Armor->Armadura Animada (cross-folder duplicate with Beasts copy, both now _id-keyed in vagabond.bestiary.json instead of the old ambiguous name-keyed stub — see landmine note in bestiary_pt_br_translation_plan memory), Crawling Wall->Parede Rastejante, Dungeonheart->Coração da Masmorra [coined], Flying Spellbook->Grimório Voador, Flying Sword->Espada Voadora, Gargoyle->Gárgula, Homunculus->Homúnculo, Joust Guardian->Guardião de Justa [coined], Necrophidius->Necrofídio [coined, PT-derivable from necro+ophidian roots, matches Owl Bear-style compound-translation precedent rather than kept-as-loanword], Ripworm->Verme Rasgador [coined], Scarecrow->Espantalho, Stone Colossus->Colosso de Pedra. Potead (Large/Medium/Small)->kept as coined proper name, same precedent as Lich/Wight/Efreeti, sizes translated via existing Grande/Médio/Pequeno lang-file terms. Not user-confirmed yet, same as prior packs.
Blink (Blink Dog ability, teleport withi: Lampejo [compendium-only, reused verbatim from the Blink spell/relic power precedent]
Blink Dog (monster name): Cão Lampejante [coined, ties to the Lampejo teleport ability]
Brownie (monster name, household prankst: Trasgo [coined — real Iberian folklore term for a mischievous house spirit, closest PT-BR equivalent]
Hag (monster name prefix): Bruxa [comma-structure kept: Hag, Green -> Bruxa, Verde / Hag, Grove -> Bruxa, Bosque / Hag, Sea -> Bruxa, Mar]
Pixie: Duende [coined — small mischievous magical fae, distinct from Trasgo/Brownie]
Pack Hunter (recurring Beast ability, al: Instintos de Matilha [precedent found already in-use on Kobold, Warrior/Alchemist abilities — same mechanic, reused verbatim rather than coining a literal translation]
Pounce (recurring predator ability, Pron: Bote [coined — PT-BR idiom "dar o bote" for an animal pounce/leap-attack]
Stomp (action): Pisão [precedent found already in-use on Jarjacha/Nightmare]
Rampant (charge-and-free-attack ability,: Arremetida [coined — distinct from Investida (Slam) and Carga (Charger)]
Blood Sense (Shark/Leech ability): Percepção de Sangue [coined, matches existing Percepção Cega/Sísmica naming family]
On-Sight (Crab ability, Berserk toward a: Fúria Instantânea [coined, matches existing Fúria Flamejante/Fúria Ferida/Sede de Sangue "Fúria"-root family; Berserk (status) confirmed = Furioso]
Drain (Leech/Weasel action, heal-by-dama: Drenar [coined, verb-infinitive style matching other maneuver-type action names]
Frost Breath (Winter Wolf action, distin: Sopro Gélido [coined, reuses Sopro/Gélido from existing Dragon-breath and Toque Gélido precedents]
Heavy (armor category, bestiary flavor u: Pesada [lang file, armor category — same word already used for the Light/Medium/Heavy category set]
Omphalos (Divine trinket item, kept untr: Omphalos [proper mythological term, cognate, kept as loanword]
Brownie (fae creature, trinket item flav: Trasgo [coined, Iberian folklore term for household fae]
Cairn (stone marker, trinket item flavor: Cairn [kept as loanword, no established PT fantasy term found]
Trinket (gear gearCategory prefix, 80-it: Trinket [kept untranslated per user 2026-07-19 — do NOT use Talismã here; that is reserved for the separate relic-context Trinket]
